edges.description: Productive infection of airway and alveolar epithelium triggers the host antiviral interferon program.

edges.description: Infection of ACE2-expressing sustentacular support cells of the olfactory epithelium disrupts olfactory function, producing loss of smell.
edges.source: SARS-CoV-2 Spike-Mediated Entry via ACE2 and TMPRSS2

edges.description: ACE2-mediated infection of oral/taste-associated epithelium contributes to loss of taste.
edges.source: SARS-CoV-2 Spike-Mediated Entry via ACE2 and TMPRSS2

edges.description: IFN-induced PARP9/DTX3L catalyzes mono-ADP-ribosylation of host substrates as an antiviral effector.
edges.source: RNA Virus-Induced Interferon and PARP Activation

edges.description: The Nsp3 Mac1 macrodomain hydrolyzes the PARP9/DTX3L-deposited mono-ADP-ribose marks on host proteins.
De-ADP-ribosylation of antiviral host substrates restores conditions permissive for viral replication and downstream tissue damage.
edges.target: Enhanced Viral Replication and Tissue Pathology

edges.description: Severe disease is marked by dysregulated cytokine responses and diffuse alveolar damage.
edges.source: Enhanced Viral Replication and Tissue Pathology

edges.description: Viral replication and inflammatory injury of the lower respiratory tract produce viral pneumonia.
edges.source: Enhanced Viral Replication and Tissue Pathology

edges.description: Infection and inflammation of the airway epithelium stimulate the cough reflex.
edges.source: Enhanced Viral Replication and Tissue Pathology

edges.description: Alveolar and interstitial lung injury impairs gas exchange, producing breathlessness.
edges.source: Enhanced Viral Replication and Tissue Pathology

edges.description: The hyperinflammatory cytokine state with diffuse alveolar damage culminates in clinical acute respiratory distress syndrome.
Pyrogenic cytokines (IL-1, IL-6, TNF-α) elevated during the cytokine storm raise the hypothalamic set-point, producing fever.
Cytokine-driven lymphocyte apoptosis and tissue redistribution contribute to the lymphopenia characteristic of acute COVID-19.

evidence.snippet: we demonstrate that SARS-CoV-2 uses the SARS-CoV receptor ACE2 for entry and the serine protease TMPRSS2 for S protein priming

nodes.description: A subset of patients with severe COVID-19 develop hypoxemic respiratory failure meeting Berlin ARDS criteria, requiring high-flow oxygen, non-invasive ventilation, or mechanical ventilation.

evidence.snippet: Complications included acute respiratory distress syndrome

nodes.description: Loss of taste commonly co-occurs with anosmia in acute COVID-19.

evidence.snippet: the proportion of participants who reported loss of smell and taste was higher in those with a positive test result

nodes.description: Sudden-onset loss of smell, often accompanied by loss of taste, is highly associated with SARS-CoV-2 infection and is the single strongest symptom predictor of a positive test in community cohorts.

evidence.snippet: the proportion of participants who reported loss of smell and taste was higher in those with a positive test result

nodes.description: Cough is among the most common presenting symptoms of acute COVID-19.

evidence.snippet: of 41 patients), cough

nodes.description: A subset of patients develops severe lower-respiratory disease with elevated inflammatory cytokines, lymphopenia, diffuse alveolar damage, and acute respiratory distress syndrome (ARDS). Markedly elevated IL-6 and other inflammatory mediators identify a hyperinflammatory state amenable to corticosteroid and anti-cytokine therapy.

evidence.snippet: Compared with non-ICU patients, ICU patients had higher plasma levels of IL2, IL7, IL10, GSCF, IP10, MCP1, MIP1A, and TNFα.
Complications included acute respiratory distress syndrome

nodes.description: Dyspnea typically develops several days after symptom onset and marks progression from upper-respiratory illness to viral pneumonia.

evidence.snippet: Dyspnoea developed in

nodes.description: By neutralizing host PARP9/DTX3L-dependent antiviral ADP-ribosylation, the Nsp3 macrodomain permits sustained viral replication in respiratory epithelium, leading to alveolar epithelial injury, diffuse alveolar damage, and recruitment of innate immune effectors. The interplay of viral cytopathic injury and dysregulated host responses drives the pneumonia, hypoxemia, and acute respiratory distress syndrome that characterize severe COVID-19.
nodes.id: Enhanced Viral Replication and Tissue Pathology

evidence.snippet: The macrodomain is essential for CoV pathogenesis and may be a novel therapeutic target.

nodes.description: Fever is the dominant presenting symptom of acute COVID-19 and was reported in 98% of hospitalized patients in the initial Wuhan cohort.

evidence.snippet: Common symptoms at onset of illness were fever

nodes.description: The interferon-inducible PARP9 ADP-ribosyltransferase, in complex with the DTX3L E3 ligase, mono-ADP-ribosylates host proteins as a downstream effector of the host antiviral interferon response. This NAD-dependent modification is the substrate that the SARS-CoV-2 Nsp3 macrodomain targets and reverses.

evidence.snippet: This IFN-induced ADP-ribosylation is dependent on PARP9 and its binding partner DTX3L

nodes.description: Reduced peripheral lymphocyte counts are commonly observed in moderate- to-severe COVID-19 and track with disease severity.

evidence.snippet: of 41 patients had lymphopenia

nodes.description: SARS-CoV-2 encodes a conserved macrodomain (Mac1) within nonstructural protein 3 (Nsp3) that binds and hydrolyzes mono-ADP-ribose marks on host proteins. Mac1 reverses the PARP9/DTX3L-dependent antiviral ADP-ribosylation that the host interferon response deposits, neutralizing this branch of innate immunity. The macrodomain is essential for coronavirus pathogenesis in animal models and is the prototypical viral pivot point for this mechanism module. Its conserved fold and druggable ADP-ribose pocket make it an attractive target for both small-molecule inhibitors and PROTAC-style degraders.

biological_processes: negative regulation of defense response to virus by host

evidence.snippet: All coronaviruses encode a highly conserved macrodomain (Mac1) that binds to and removes ADP-ribose adducts from proteins
Mac1 is essential for pathogenesis in multiple animal models of CoV infection
ectopic expression of the SARS-CoV-2 Nsp3 macrodomain reverses this modification in human cells
the cellular function of the SARS-CoV-2 Nsp3 macrodomain is to hydrolyze this end product of IFN signaling, rather than to suppress the IFN response itself

nodes.description: Pegylated interferon lambda-1A is an innate-immunity-modulator (a type III interferon) that pharmacologically augments the host interferon-stimulated antiviral state. In the TOGETHER randomized trial, a single subcutaneous dose given early to outpatients significantly reduced COVID-19 hospitalization or emergency-department visits, with benefit consistent across SARS-CoV-2 variants — the broad-spectrum, host-directed signature of an interferon-based antiviral.
Bilateral viral pneumonia with ground-glass opacities on chest imaging is the canonical lower-respiratory manifestation of moderate-to-severe acute COVID-19.

evidence.snippet: All 41 patients had pneumonia with abnormal findings on chest CT.

nodes.description: SARS-CoV-2 replication is sensed by pattern-recognition receptors that induce type I and type III interferon signaling in infected respiratory epithelium. Interferon signaling drives expression of PARP family members (including PARP9 paired with the DTX3L E3 ligase) that catalyze antiviral ADP-ribosylation as part of the innate antiviral program.
nodes.id: RNA Virus-Induced Interferon and PARP Activation

biological_processes: positive regulation of defense response to virus by host

evidence.snippet: activation of the IFN response induces ADP-ribosylation of host proteins

nodes.description: SARS-CoV-2 enters host cells via its spike (S) glycoprotein binding the cellular receptor ACE2, followed by S protein priming by the host serine protease TMPRSS2. ACE2 and TMPRSS2 co-expression on nasal, conducting airway, and alveolar epithelial cells (particularly ciliated cells and type II pneumocytes) defines the principal cellular tropism that drives upper- and lower-respiratory disease.
nodes.id: SARS-CoV-2 Spike-Mediated Entry via ACE2 and TMPRSS2

evidence.snippet: we demonstrate that SARS-CoV-2 uses the SARS-CoV receptor ACE2 for entry and the serine protease TMPRSS2 for S protein priming
A TMPRSS2 inhibitor approved for clinical use blocked entry and might constitute a treatment option
